title: Efficient Longest Increasing Subsequence with Edge Case Handling

statement.markdown:
Given an integer array $nums$ of size $n$, find the length of the longest increasing subsequence (LIS). The LIS problem is to find the length of the longest subsequence that is strictly increasing. A subsequence of an array is a new array which is formed from the data elements of the original array. The elements in the new array should be a subset of the elements in the original array, but the order and the number of times they appear can be different. The following example illustrates this: [10, 9, 2, 5, 3, 7, 101, 18]
In the above array, the LIS would be [2, 3, 7, 101]. The length of this subsequence is 4, which is the longest possible length for an increasing subsequence in the given array.
statement.inputFormat: An integer array $nums$ of size $n$ where $n$ is between 1 and $10^4$.
statement.outputFormat: The length of the longest increasing subsequence (LIS).

examples.explanation: There is only one subsequence, which is [10].
